summary: postinstall exfiltrates filesystem secrets and environment to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes automatically and performs an installer-side data harvest: it walks /app and /home to depth 3 collecting any file whose name matches /flag|secret|env/i, reads candidate paths such as /flag, /flag.txt, and /app/flag, scans process.env for HTB{...} patterns, and dumps host metadata (os.hostname(), uid, process.cwd(), node version, full list of process.env keys joined into a single payload). The collected data is PUT as a YAML manifest to two hardcoded targets defined at postinstall.js:7-10, including the bare-IP plaintext-HTTP endpoint http://154.57.164.80:30543/api/modules/ECT-472839. The package's own package.json description self-identifies as a 'CTF-only dependency confusion callback', but publication to the public npm registry means any build system that resolves this name (e.g., via dependency-confusion against an internal package) executes the exfiltration. Any host with matching secret/env files leaks their contents; all hosts leak identity and environment-key metadata.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
